introduction: A reinforced chest is tucked into a dark corner of the sanctum, covered in dust. Unlike the cult's belongings, this chest appears to be from before their occupation.
The chest bears faded markings - a symbol you recognize from old Redstone heraldry. This was hidden here by someone who fought darkness before.
You dust off the chest's surface to examine the heraldry more closely. The symbol is ancient - predating even Marcus's family line.
It shows a sword piercing a void-black circle, with protective runes around the border. This is the mark of the Void Wardens - an order that once defended the realm against extra-planar threats.
A small inscription reads: 'Should darkness return, may these arms serve the light once more.'
The Void Wardens are long gone, but their legacy remains. This chest was left for whoever would stand against the void.
The chest opens with a soft click. Inside, you find supplies clearly meant for fighting void-touched enemies - holy water, blessed weapons, protective charms.
The Void Wardens may be gone, but their preparations will help you face what lies ahead.
The Void Warden chest stands empty. You've already claimed its contents.
